beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, check plants on balcony.
beats.reasoning: Early riser habit; sunny morning perfect for checking urban garden setup before work commute.
Standard commute from Mercer Island to workplace; early arrival allows prep time before opening shift.

beats.activity: Shelving, cataloging, reference desk prep.; Patron services and reference desk.; Continue library duties, lunch break with a sandwich at desk.; Afternoon reference desk, cataloging, shelving returns.
beats.reasoning: Morning work routine; library opens at 10am, so early arrival handles backlog and opening procedures.
Evening commute home; sunny weather makes the drive pleasant.

beats.activity: Water garden beds, tend to seedlings.
beats.reasoning: Golden hour on a sunny day is perfect for urban gardening; Hiro prioritizes this personal passion.

beats.activity: Prepare and eat dinner, write a letter by hand.
beats.reasoning: Dinner and handwritten correspondence align with Hiro's preference for analog activities and reflective evening routine.
Evening wind-down with vinyl collecting hobby; quiet, solo activity suits Hiro's preference for close-friends-over-crowds lifestyle.
8.5-hour overnight sleep; early riser prepares for next day; ends before 06:30 wake-up window.

diary: Woke up early to check on my balcony plants—the tomatoes are finally getting decent height. The drive to Kirkland was smooth, which felt lucky given the Light Rail mess downtown. Work was steady: mostly shelving and reference desk, nothing too demanding. I ate lunch at my desk like always, just a sandwich and some quiet time before the afternoon rush hit. The weather was gorgeous, so I made sure to get home with enough daylight left to water the garden beds and fuss over the seedlings. Made a proper dinner for once instead of reheating, and I actually sat down to write that letter I've been meaning to send. Finished the evening with my vinyl collection, reorganizing a few albums. The whole day had this unhurried quality to it—didn't catch the World Cup match downtown, but honestly, I was content right here.
thought_process: The sunny weather pulled me toward home and my garden rather than joining the downtown buzz around the USA-Wales game. I think I needed the quietness after weeks of steady library traffic. The Light Rail closure didn't affect me since I drive from Mercer Island, but I was aware of it—noticed fewer people asking for transit directions. My mood shifted positively once I got home; the garden work grounded me in a way the library sometimes doesn't. Writing that letter felt overdue, like I'd been carrying it around mentally. The vinyl organizing was just meditative fiddling, but it felt right. Early sleep felt earned.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
